action.hypothesis: Under the fixed EF=16 regime, the simpler non-routed two-stage soft-codebook backbone may outperform the routed variant at K=16 because routing appears brittle to recipe changes while the base two-stage path has been the stronger mid-K workhorse.
action.summary: Severe stagnation guidance says to stop minor tweaks and try a fundamentally different architecture. The last two recipe-only changes on the routed K=16 incumbent failed badly, so the next clean move is an architecture switch within the existing registered families. I am testing `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at K=16 with the stable signum recipe to see whether removing routing yields a better small-K tradeoff under the new fixed EF=16 policy.
action.self_review: This satisfies the severe-stagnation requirement by changing architecture rather than applying another small recipe tweak. The main risk is that EF=16 regime drift still dominates the result, but this is a better diagnostic than continuing to perturb the clearly fragile routed K=16 setup.
notes_to_memory: Round 124 stops minor recipe tweaks after Hadamard and Adam both strongly degraded the routed K=16 incumbent.
Severe-stagnation policy now points to a fundamentally different existing architecture rather than more optimizer/preprocessing perturbations on the same family.
Test `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at K=16 as the simplest strong soft-codebook backbone to check whether routing is the source of fragility under EF=16.
next_hypotheses: If the non-routed two-stage soft-codebook model improves at K=16, follow with K=32 under the same EF=16 recipe to map whether the simpler backbone is broadly better in the fixed-EF regime.
If it still underperforms badly, test a more structurally different existing family such as `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at K=16 rather than returning to recipe tweaks.

If multiple existing architectures all regress under EF=16, prioritize diagnosing fixed-EF regime shift with a direct EF16 control narrative instead of attributing the failures to optimizer or preprocessing alone.

I am testing `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at K=16 with the stable signum recipe to see whether removing routing yields a better small-K tradeoff under the new fixed EF=16 policy.
